title: Executive Director
description: Lead DAT's organizational operations, financial strategy, and institutional partnerships. The ED partners with the Artistic Director to drive the company's growth at a pivotal moment in its evolution.
long_description:
As DAT completes its 20th season — twenty years of artist-led, community-rooted international theatre-making — we need an Executive Director who can hold the organization while we reach for what's next.

We've built Drama Clubs in Slovakia and Ecuador, mounted ACTion expeditions to Tanzania, Czechia, and beyond, grown an alumni network spanning continents, and developed a storytelling platform rooted in real community voices. Now we're building the infrastructure to scale thoughtfully and sustain what we've made.

This is a rare seat: direct partnership with the Artistic Director, meaningful creative proximity, and real institutional work. The ED will oversee operations, finance, development infrastructure, partnerships, and staff — and will help shape what DAT becomes over the next five years.
what_youll_do: Oversee organizational operations, financial management, budgeting, and reporting
Manage and grow a lean staff team and network of contractors and PLX alumni
Drive institutional fundraising strategy alongside the Development team
Build and steward partnerships with funders, government bodies, universities, and community organizations
Serve as the primary liaison to the Board of Directors
Lead HR, compliance, and legal/organizational matters
Collaborate with the Artistic Director on strategic planning and season-level decisions
who_you_are: A builder — you've run organizations or departments before and you know what good systems feel like
Comfortable in financial complexity — budgets, reporting, and sustainability planning
A collaborative partner — you bring out the best in creative colleagues without needing to be the creative one
Committed to DAT's values: cross-cultural exchange, community rootedness, artistic rigor
Low-ego, high-ownership, ready to roll up your sleeves
requirements: 5+ years of nonprofit leadership or equivalent experience
Financial management experience — budgets, cash flow, audit-readiness
Strong background in fundraising, grant management, or development infrastructure
Proven experience managing staff or teams
US-based or willing to work US hours
Ability to travel internationally at least once per year
perks: Compensation range shared with qualified applicants
Open to full-time, fractional, or phased arrangements depending on fit and funding
Remote-first; in-person presence expected for key organizational moments
Creative proximity to a working international theatre company
Benefits and travel expectations discussed during the offer process

faq.a: We share the range directly with qualified applicants. Write to jobs@dramaticadventure.com to learn more.
Yes. The ED reports to the Board and works closely with the Artistic Director.
Core team is distributed. Quarterly in-person gatherings and annual international expedition travel.

title: Development & Partnerships Lead
description: Build and steward DAT's funding base and institutional relationships. Lead grant strategy, major-donor cultivation, and the partnerships that sustain DAT's international work.
compensation: Salary or contract rate commensurate with experience
long_description:
DAT has a loyal donor base, a compelling story, and a track record of producing work across three continents. What we need is someone who can build the infrastructure around all that — a Development & Partnerships Lead who treats fundraising as a craft, not a chore.

This role is open to a full-time hire or a fractional/contract arrangement for the right person. You'll drive our grant calendar, deepen individual-donor relationships, and build the institutional connections that make international theatre possible.
what_youll_do: Lead DAT's annual grant calendar — research, write, track, report
Cultivate and steward a portfolio of individual major donors
Identify and pursue new institutional partnerships (universities, foundations, government arts agencies, corporate sponsors)
Manage our development database and donor communications cadence
Draft annual-fund campaigns, year-end appeals, and campaign-specific donor materials
Represent DAT at key partnership meetings and convenings
Collaborate with Comms on donor-facing storytelling
who_you_are: An experienced development professional with a strong writing portfolio
Comfortable asking for money and comfortable cultivating the relationship before the ask
Organized, deadline-driven, and genuinely curious about DAT's work
Able to work independently and communicate proactively
requirements: Proficient with a CRM (Salesforce NPSP or similar)
perks: Compensation commensurate with experience (salary or contract)
Meaningful mission with direct creative proximity
Opportunity to travel to a DAT project in the field (subject to production calendar and funding)

title: Managing Producer / Program Operations Lead
description: Keep DAT's multi-continent program engine running. The Managing Producer owns logistics, timelines, budgets, and coordination across productions, residencies, and program cycles.
compensation: Salary or contract rate commensurate with experience
long_description:
DAT's work runs across multiple countries, programs, and time zones simultaneously. Someone has to hold the map.

The Managing Producer is the operational core of the company: tracking timelines, managing program budgets, coordinating artists and staff, and making sure the productions, residencies, ACTion expeditions, and Drama Club seasons all stay on track and on budget. This is not a desk job. It's a craft role for someone who loves the systems that make creative work possible.
what_youll_do: Manage production and program timelines across all active DAT projects
Oversee logistics for ACTion expeditions — travel, housing, visas, on-the-ground coordination
Build and track program budgets in partnership with the ED
Coordinate artists, staff, contractors, and local partners across productions
Manage the PLX program calendar and mentorship schedules
Keep project documentation current — reports, contracts, tracking sheets
Identify and problem-solve operational bottlenecks before they become crises
who_you_are: A producer by instinct — you see the whole board and the next three moves
Comfortable with international logistics and the chaos that comes with them
Detail-oriented without losing the big picture
Calm under pressure, generous in a crunch
requirements: 3+ years producing theatre, events, or international programs
Strong project-management skills (Asana, Notion, or similar)
Ability to travel internationally 1–2 times per year
perks: Creative proximity to a working international theatre company
Opportunity to travel to a DAT project in the field (subject to production calendar and funding)

title: Communications & Story Lead
description: Shape how DAT tells its story to the world. Lead content strategy, editorial voice, social media, and the communications infrastructure that connects our work to audiences, donors, and partners.
compensation: Salary or contract rate commensurate with experience
long_description:
After twenty seasons of artist-led, community-rooted international theatre-making, DAT has more stories to tell than infrastructure to tell them. It's time to build that.

The Communications & Story Lead will shape the editorial and content infrastructure that lets DAT's work speak — across social media, the alumni storytelling platform, donor communications, press, and digital campaigns. This is a role for a writer-thinker who can hold DAT's voice with care while moving fast enough to capture the work as it happens.
what_youll_do: Own DAT's editorial voice across all public channels
Build and maintain a content calendar tied to the season, programs, and fundraising cycles
Write and produce content for social (IG, TikTok, LinkedIn), email, and the alumni storytelling platform
Lead press outreach and media relations
Draft donor-facing narratives in partnership with Development
Coach teaching artists and field staff on documentation and content capture
Build and maintain DAT's communications infrastructure (email platform, templates, social scheduling)
who_you_are: A strong writer with a distinctive voice — you write like a human, not a press release
Fluent in social-content thinking, especially short-form video and editorial IG
Organized enough to build systems, scrappy enough to not need perfect conditions
Genuinely excited by DAT's story
requirements: 3+ years in communications, content, journalism, or related field
Email platform experience (Mailchimp, Klaviyo, or similar)
perks: Opportunity to travel to a DAT project in the field (subject to production calendar and funding)

title: Bookkeeper / Finance Admin
description: Keep DAT's books clean, accurate, and audit-ready. Support the ED and Managing Producer with financial reporting, expense tracking, and grant financial documentation.
compensation: Contract rate TBD; hourly or monthly retainer
long_description: DAT needs a part-time bookkeeper to own our financial hygiene — monthly reconciliation, expense categorization, payroll support, and clean reporting for grants and the Board. This is a fractional/contract role, ideal for a bookkeeper with nonprofit experience who manages a small portfolio of clients.
what_youll_do: Monthly bank and credit card reconciliation
Categorize expenses by program and grant
Prepare financial reports for the ED and Board
Support grant financial reporting (actuals vs. budget)
Coordinate with payroll processor
Flag compliance concerns or irregularities
who_you_are: Detail-oriented and reliable
Comfortable with nonprofit fund accounting
Discreet and trustworthy with financial information

title: Local Project Producer
description: Lead local production logistics for a specific DAT project — from an ACTion expedition to a Drama Club season or community residency. Project-based role tied to a specific geography and timeline.
long_description:
DAT's next wave of projects — PASSAGE Slovakia 2026, Drama Club expansions, new community residencies — each needs a producer on the ground who knows the territory.

The Local Project Producer is a project-based contract role: you come in for a specific project, own the local logistics, and leave with a strong credit and a standing invitation to come back. We're building a bench of project producers across our active hubs as DAT builds toward Season 21 and beyond.
what_youll_do: Own logistics for a specific DAT project in your city or region
Coordinate with local partners, venues, housing, and transport
Manage the local project budget and timeline
Serve as primary liaison between DAT HQ and on-the-ground reality
Support artist arrivals, orientations, and day-to-day production needs
Document the project for DAT's records and storytelling channels
who_you_are: Based in or deeply familiar with the project hub (Slovakia, Ecuador, NYC, etc.)
A practical, resourceful producer — you've managed logistics under pressure
Warm, bridge-building energy
Familiar with local arts, cultural, or community organizations
requirements: 2+ years of producing or event/project management experience
Based in or able to relocate to the project hub for the duration
References from prior production or community work
perks: First right of refusal on future projects in your hub

timeline.label: Other Hubs
timeline.detail: Inquire; we'll match you when the right project opens

title: Slovakia 2026 — Local Producer / Ground Coordinator
description: Own the on-the-ground logistics for PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 (July 12–August 2). You know Slovakia — its venues, transport, hospitality, and arts landscape. Project-based contract; urgent hire.
compensation: Project-based contract fee; rate shared with qualified applicants
long_description:
PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 runs July 12–August 2, moving through Bratislava, Košice, Zemplínska Teplica, and Slovenský Raj. We need someone on the ground in Slovakia who can own the local logistics — venues, housing, transport, local partnerships, and day-to-day production reality.

This is an urgent, time-specific role. Pre-production begins immediately; in-field dates are July 12–August 2. We need someone who knows Slovakia, can move quickly, and has a producer’s instinct for solving problems before they become crises.
what_youll_do: Secure and confirm venues, housing, and transport for all stops on the route
Serve as primary local contact for DAT HQ throughout pre-production and in-field
Coordinate with local community partners, arts organizations, and cultural contacts
Manage the local project budget and logistics timeline
Support artist arrivals, orientations, and day-to-day production needs in the field
Document logistics decisions and local context for DAT’s records
who_you_are: Based in Slovakia or with deep working knowledge of the country
A practical, resourceful producer — you’ve managed logistics under pressure
Fluent or highly functional in Slovak (English also required)
Connected to local arts, cultural, or community organizations
Available to commit to the full pre-production and in-field window
requirements: Based in Slovakia or able to be fully present there from June onward
2+ years of producing, event management, or project coordination experience
Strong working knowledge of at least two route regions: Bratislava, Košice, Zemplínska Teplica, or Slovenský Raj
References from prior production or community work
Fluency or working proficiency in Slovak
perks: Project-based contract fee (rate discussed with qualified applicants)
Housing support during in-field period where applicable
First right of refusal on future DAT projects in Slovakia or Central Europe

faq.a: Very. PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 runs July 12–August 2. Pre-production needs to begin in June. Please apply now if you’re interested.
Yes. Project-based contract fee; rate shared directly with qualified applicants.

title: Participant & Donor Coordinator
description: Manage communications and relationships with program participants, travelers, and donors. Own the inbox, CRM hygiene, and the day-to-day connections that keep DAT's community close.
compensation: Contract rate TBD; hourly or monthly
long_description:
DAT's community is its greatest asset — artists, donors, travelers, Drama Club families, alumni, volunteers. The Participant & Donor Coordinator is the warm human voice that keeps these relationships alive between programs.

You'll manage communications workflows, CRM records, and the day-to-day touchpoints that make people feel seen and appreciated.
what_youll_do: Manage participant and donor communications workflows and templates
Maintain CRM records — data entry, segmentation, tagging
Coordinate donor acknowledgements, gift processing, and tax receipts
Support program enrollment communications for ACTion, Drama Clubs, and PLX
Assist with event RSVPs, follow-up, and logistics
Flag relationship opportunities and at-risk donor signals to the Development team
who_you_are: Warm, organized, and genuinely people-oriented
Comfortable managing a busy inbox and a CRM simultaneously
Takes pride in clear, friendly communication
Good at reading between the lines of a donor or participant relationship
requirements: 1–2 years in donor relations, customer service, program coordination, or similar

title: Fundraising Ambassador
description: Help DAT grow its individual-donor base by sharing the story with your network. No cold calling — just warm connections and a genuine belief in what DAT does.
long_description: DAT's most effective fundraisers are the people who've been changed by the work — alumni, travelers, Drama Club families, friends. The Fundraising Ambassador formalizes what many of you already do: tell the story, make the introduction, invite someone into the community. We'll give you the tools, the talking points, and the support.
what_youll_do: Identify 5–10 people in your network who might connect with DAT
Share DAT's story through personal outreach or social media
Invite prospects to events, the alumni platform, or a direct conversation
Participate in a monthly Ambassadors call (optional but encouraged)
Report back on connections made
who_you_are: A genuine DAT believer — you've seen the work, been part of it, or been moved by it
Comfortable having personal conversations about causes you care about
Connected in arts, education, philanthropy, or community
requirements: DAT alumni or close community member preferred
perks: Direct briefings from Jesse on DAT's strategy and vision
Invitations to donor events and salons
First look at new programs and projects

timeline.label: Monthly Ambassadors Call
timeline.detail: Optional; first Monday of the month

title: Alumni Outreach Captain
description: Help DAT reconnect with lost alumni, re-engage lapsed community members, and keep the network alive and growing. A role for someone with strong relationship instincts and a long memory.
long_description: DAT has thousands of alumni across every continent. Many haven't heard from us in years. The Alumni Outreach Captain helps us change that — making personal connections, sharing news, and inviting people back into the fold. This is a warm, relationship-first role for someone who loves people and understands what it means to belong to something.
what_youll_do: Identify alumni in your region or cohort who've gone quiet
Reach out personally — a message, an email, an introduction
Invite alumni to update their stories on the alumni platform
Flag high-engagement alumni who might be ready for a deeper role
Contribute to a quarterly Alumni Network report
who_you_are: A natural connector
A DAT alumnus or close community member with strong network instincts
Warm, persistent, and genuinely interested in people
requirements: Comfortable with email outreach and social media
perks: Invitations to alumni events and salons
Quarterly briefings from the DAT team

title: Story Collector
description: Gather stories from DAT alumni, participants, and community members for the alumni storytelling platform and campaigns. A role for interviewers, writers, and people who love listening.
long_description: The alumni storytelling platform at stories.dramaticadventure.com is only as rich as the stories it holds. Story Collectors conduct short interviews with DAT alumni and participants, transcribe or summarize them, and hand them off to the Comms team for publishing. It's close listening work — and it matters.
what_youll_do: Conduct 1–2 story interviews per month (30–45 min, video or phone)
Transcribe or summarize interview content for the editorial team
Coordinate with the Comms team on story priorities and upcoming campaign themes
Keep a simple tracking log of interviews conducted and stories submitted
who_you_are: A good listener first
A writer or interviewer who knows how to draw people out
Patient and warm, genuinely curious about other people's experiences
requirements: Comfortable conducting video or phone interviews
perks: Byline credit on published stories (by arrangement)
Deep access to DAT's extraordinary community

title: Event Volunteer
description: Support DAT events in New York — front of house, hospitality, setup, and audience experience. A great way to stay connected to the work and meet the community.
long_description:
DAT runs 20–30 events a year in NYC — performances, salons, fundraisers, community nights, donor gatherings. Behind every one is a team of volunteers who make the room feel alive. This is the entry point for many people into DAT's wider community.

Flexible commitment — come to one event or become a regular. Most volunteers do 2–4 events a season.
what_youll_do: Help with setup and breakdown
Greet audiences and check in ticket holders
Support hospitality — atmosphere, flow, guest experience
Help with merch and program distribution
Act as audience-experience host during shows
who_you_are: Friendly, reliable, and easy to be around
Comfortable on your feet for 3–4 hours
Love live performance and want to be near it
requirements: 18 years or older (16+ for non-alcohol events)
Available for at least one event per season
perks: Complimentary tickets to the event you work
Free tickets to two other DAT events per season
Dinner with the team at quarterly volunteer nights
First look at auditions and PLX opportunities

title: Grant Research Volunteer
description: Identify new grant and funding opportunities for DAT. Research foundations, government programs, and corporate sponsors that align with DAT's international, community-rooted mission.
long_description: DAT's Development team can't read every RFP. The Grant Research Volunteer helps fill the gap — scanning foundations, government programs, and arts funding databases for opportunities that match our work. You'll submit a monthly research brief that the Development team uses to prioritize its grant calendar.
what_youll_do: Research 3–5 new grant prospects per month using GrantStation, Foundation Directory, NEA, state arts councils, and other sources
Summarize each prospect in a short brief (eligibility, deadline, fit, notes)
Flag high-priority opportunities to the Development team
Maintain a running prospect tracker
who_you_are: Methodical and curious
Comfortable with database research
Interested in arts funding, nonprofit finance, or development as a field
perks: Briefings from DAT's Development team on grant strategy

title: Data / CRM Volunteer
description: Help DAT maintain clean, organized, and useful data across our donor, alumni, and participant databases. Ideal for someone with spreadsheet or CRM experience who wants to contribute with precision.
long_description: Clean data is the unglamorous foundation of everything DAT does — from donor stewardship to alumni engagement to grant reporting. The Data/CRM Volunteer helps us maintain it: deduplicating records, updating contact information, tagging and segmenting, and flagging inconsistencies.
what_youll_do: Audit and clean CRM records on a monthly basis
Update contact info, tags, and segmentation
Deduplicate alumni and donor records
Assist with data imports after events, campaigns, or program cycles
Flag systemic data issues to the Coordinator
who_you_are: Detail-oriented and careful
Comfortable with spreadsheets and CRMs
Not bothered by repetitive precision work

title: Program Recruitment Ambassador
description: Help DAT find the next wave of ACTion participants, Drama Club students, PLX applicants, and traveling artists. Share opportunities in your community, on your campus, or through your professional network.
long_description: DAT's programs fill from word of mouth. The Program Recruitment Ambassador formalizes that — sharing application deadlines, program descriptions, and personal recommendations with communities that should know about DAT's work. University contacts, community centers, arts organizations, theatre departments — your network is our pipeline.
what_youll_do: Share DAT opportunity announcements with your network, on campus, or in your community
Identify organizations and institutions to add to DAT's outreach list
Post or share program deadlines on social media
Participate in a quarterly recruitment check-in call
who_you_are: Plugged into a community where DAT's programs would resonate
Enthusiastic about DAT's mission
Natural sharer
requirements: Active in a relevant community or institution
perks: First look at new program announcements

title: Travel & Logistics Research Volunteer
description: Support DAT's producing team by researching housing, transport, visa requirements, venues, and logistics for upcoming expeditions and residencies.
long_description: Planning an international theatre expedition requires a lot of ground-level research — housing options, local transport, visa requirements, cultural considerations, local contacts. The Travel & Logistics Research Volunteer helps our producing team build the picture before the decisions get made. Project-based, you'll be assigned specific research tasks with clear deliverables and deadlines.
what_youll_do: Research housing options for a specific expedition (DAT typically houses 6–15 artists)
Map transport logistics — airport transfers, local travel, vehicle hire
Summarize visa requirements for a mixed-nationality ensemble
Identify potential local partners, venues, or service providers
Submit a structured research brief to the Managing Producer
who_you_are: Methodical, resourceful, comfortable with international research
May have experience with travel logistics, event production, or study abroad
requirements: Comfortable with online research in English and other languages (with tools)
Available for a 2–3 week project window
perks: Insight into the real infrastructure of international theatre-making

timeline.label: Project-based
timeline.detail: Assigned as expedition planning windows open

title: Community Partner Liaison
description: Help DAT understand the community landscape in cities where we work. A connector role for people with strong local ties who want to support DAT’s relationship-building — without owning decisions.
long_description: DAT’s partnerships are the arteries of the organization — local drama clubs, community centers, universities, cultural organizations, arts agencies. The Community Partner Liaison helps us understand and navigate those landscapes in cities and regions where we work or plan to work. DAT staff own the formal relationship-building and any institutional commitments; the Liaison helps us see clearly. You know the landscape; we’ll give you the context.
what_youll_do: Research and surface relevant organizations, venues, and community contacts in your hub
Attend local arts or community events and share what you learn with the DAT team
Keep a simple running log of promising relationships and local context
Pass along warm introductions between people in your network and DAT staff
Share occasional updates on the arts and community landscape in your region
who_you_are: Deeply embedded in your local arts, education, or community sector
A natural bridge-builder with a warm reputation
Patient with relationship timelines
requirements: Strong local network in a city or region relevant to DAT
Comfortable representing an organization in community settings
perks: Invitations to relevant DAT programs in your region

title: Development Intern
description: Support DAT's fundraising and grant strategy. Research prospects, assist with grant writing, maintain donor records, and learn the craft of development inside a global arts nonprofit.
long_description: The Development Intern works alongside DAT's Development team to support grant research, writing, donor communications, and CRM maintenance. You'll learn the mechanics of arts fundraising from the inside — deadlines, relationships, the ask, the story.
what_youll_do: Research new grant and funding prospects
Assist in drafting grant narratives and reports
Maintain and clean the donor CRM
Draft donor acknowledgements and communications
Support annual-fund and campaign logistics
Participate in weekly Development team meetings
who_you_are: Interested in arts fundraising, nonprofit finance, or development as a career path
Strong writer, organized, deadline-driven
Curious about how theatre gets funded
requirements: Enrolled in or recently graduated from a degree program
perks: Portfolio of real grant and donor work

faq.a: No. We'll teach you. Bring curiosity and a strong writing sample.

title: Communications & Storytelling Intern
description: Help tell DAT's story across social media, the alumni platform, email, and editorial content. A great fit for writers, content creators, and aspiring communications professionals.
long_description: The Communications & Storytelling Intern works with DAT's Comms team to draft, produce, and publish content across social media, email, and the alumni storytelling platform. You'll write in DAT's voice, edit alumni stories, draft social copy, and help build the editorial calendar.
what_youll_do: Draft social media posts across IG, TikTok, and LinkedIn
Write and edit alumni stories for the storytelling platform
Assist with email newsletter drafts
Support content calendar maintenance
Conduct story interviews with alumni or participants (optional)
Contribute to campaign content as needed
who_you_are: A writer with a distinct voice
Comfortable in social media content creation
Genuinely curious about DAT's work and community
Detail-oriented about tone and accuracy

title: Program Operations Intern
description: Support the logistics and operations of DAT's programs — ACTion, Drama Clubs, PLX, and residencies. A great fit for aspiring producers and arts administrators who love the systems behind the work.
long_description: The Program Operations Intern supports the producing and program team with the logistics that keep DAT's work moving — tracking timelines, managing documentation, coordinating communications, and supporting program delivery across multiple active projects.
what_youll_do: Maintain project trackers and documentation
Support logistics coordination for active programs
Assist with scheduling and communications across teams
Help with program enrollment and participant onboarding
Research tools, vendors, and logistics solutions as needed
Participate in weekly team meetings
who_you_are: Organized and proactive
Interested in arts administration, producing, or nonprofit management
Comfortable working across multiple projects simultaneously
requirements: Enrolled in or recently graduated from a degree program
Comfortable with project management tools (Notion, Asana, Google Workspace)

title: Alumni & Community Engagement Intern
description: Help DAT stay connected to its global alumni community. Support outreach, events, the storytelling platform, and the relationships that keep the DAT network alive.
long_description: The Alumni & Community Engagement Intern supports DAT's relationship with its global alumni network — helping with outreach, event logistics, the alumni storytelling platform, and the communications that keep people connected to each other and to the organization.
what_youll_do: Support alumni outreach campaigns and re-engagement efforts
Help maintain the alumni database and storytelling platform
Assist with community event logistics (virtual and NYC-based)
Conduct alumni check-in calls or surveys
Draft alumni communications and updates
Contribute to quarterly alumni newsletters
who_you_are: People-oriented, warm, and organized
Interested in community building, alumni relations, or nonprofit engagement
Comfortable doing outreach and relationship management
requirements: Enrolled in or recently graduated from a degree program
Comfortable with email outreach and CRM tools
perks: Deep access to DAT's global community

title: Digital / Website Intern
description: Support the maintenance, updates, and improvement of DAT's digital properties — the main site, the alumni storytelling platform, and digital assets. A great fit for web-savvy students with an interest in arts and nonprofits.
long_description: The Digital/Website Intern helps DAT's small team maintain and improve its digital presence. You might help update alumni profiles, fix small UX issues, improve page content, support SEO, or build simple reporting dashboards. This is a hands-on role for someone comfortable with web tools and willing to learn.
what_youll_do: Help maintain and update website content and alumni profiles
Support small front-end improvements or content updates
Assist with digital asset organization
Research SEO and accessibility improvements
Build simple reporting tools or dashboards
Test and document bugs or UX issues
who_you_are: Web-comfortable — HTML/CSS, CMS tools, or basic Next.js experience welcome
Detail-oriented and methodical
Interested in arts, nonprofits, or digital storytelling
requirements: Experience with at least one CMS or web platform

title: Production Intern
description: Support the production logistics and documentation of DAT's active projects — from design coordination to schedules to production research. A great fit for aspiring production managers and all-around theatre-makers.
long_description: The Production Intern supports DAT's producing team with the technical and logistical work of mounting theatre in unusual places. You might research venue options in Slovakia, coordinate with a set designer in Ecuador, maintain a production calendar for a NYC performance, or help document a Drama Club residency. The work varies. The rigor doesn't.
what_youll_do: Support production timelines and technical coordination across active projects
Research venues, equipment, and vendors for upcoming productions
Assist with production documentation — schedules, contact sheets, run-of-show
Help coordinate with designers and technical staff
Attend production and design meetings (remote)
Contribute to post-production documentation and wrap reports
who_you_are: Interested in production, stage management, or technical theatre
Organized, detail-oriented, calm under pressure
Genuinely curious about how theatre gets made in unconventional spaces
requirements: Background or coursework in theatre production, stage management, or technical theatre preferred
Comfortable with remote coordination and documentation tools

title: Research & Dramaturgy Intern
description: Support the dramaturgical and research needs of DAT's active productions and residencies. Dig into history, context, and source material to help DAT's artists make richer, more grounded work.
long_description: The Research & Dramaturgy Intern works with DAT's artistic team to dig into the historical, cultural, and textual context of our current projects. For Slovakia 2026, this might mean researching Roma cultural history, Velvet Revolution theatre, and contemporary Czech civil society. For an Ecuador-based piece, it might mean indigenous Kichwa storytelling traditions. The work is specific; the stakes are real.
what_youll_do: Conduct dramaturgical and contextual research for active productions
Compile and summarize research into accessible briefs for the artistic team
Identify relevant source texts, historical events, and cultural context
Support adaptation and script development research as needed
Contribute to the production's archive and documentation
Participate in regular artistic team check-ins
who_you_are: A researcher with intellectual rigor and genuine curiosity
Background in theatre studies, history, cultural studies, anthropology, or a related field
Comfortable with ambiguity and open-ended inquiry
requirements: Enrolled in or recently graduated from a relevant degree program
Comfortable working with primary and secondary sources

title: Producing Apprentice
description: A 10-month producing apprenticeship for early-career arts administrators. Work alongside DAT's producing team, lead real projects, and gain deep hands-on experience in international theatre production.
long_description: The Producing Apprentice is a core member of DAT's producing team for the season — owning a real portfolio of work, managing logistics, supporting productions across multiple hubs, and building a producing career from the inside out. This is not a shadow experience. You'll have responsibility, autonomy, and a team behind you.
what_youll_do: Own a producing portfolio across one or more active DAT programs
Manage logistics for an ACTion expedition or residency
Lead coordination with artists, local partners, and vendors
Support the Managing Producer with timeline and budget tracking
Travel with DAT on at least one in-field expedition
Mentor PLX Interns in your department
who_you_are: 1–3 years of producing, stage management, or arts admin experience
Action-oriented and resourceful
Comfortable with international logistics
Ready to take real ownership of real work
requirements: Willing to travel internationally at least once
References from prior producing or arts admin work

title: Teaching Artist Apprentice
description: A 10-month apprenticeship for artists developing their teaching practice. Co-lead Drama Club sessions, design curriculum, and build your pedagogy inside a community-engaged theatre company.
long_description: The Teaching Artist Apprentice works with DAT's education team to develop their practice — co-leading Drama Club sessions, designing curriculum, learning DAT's devising and storytelling pedagogy, and eventually leading sessions independently. This apprenticeship builds a strong foundation for a career as a community-engaged teaching artist.
what_youll_do: Co-lead DAT Drama Club sessions with a mentor teaching artist
Design and test curriculum for youth and community settings
Develop your personal teaching artist statement and toolkit
Lead at least one independent session per semester
Participate in teaching artist training workshops
Travel to or observe at least one international residency
who_you_are: An artist with a genuine interest in teaching, community, and pedagogy
Patient, warm, and energized by working with young people and communities
In the early stages of your teaching artist career
requirements: Background as a performer, director, musician, or teaching artist
Willing to travel for at least one residency observation

title: Community Engagement Apprentice
description: A 10-month apprenticeship for practitioners building community-engagement and partnership skills. Work alongside DAT's program team to build, deepen, and document community relationships across DAT's active hubs.
long_description: The Community Engagement Apprentice builds DAT's relationships with communities — facilitating listening sessions, managing partner communications, supporting local producers, and helping translate DAT's mission into local partnerships that are authentic and equitable. This apprenticeship is for someone who wants to make community engagement a craft, not a checkbox.
what_youll_do: Manage communications with community partners in one or more DAT hubs
Co-facilitate community listening sessions and workshops
Support local producers and community liaisons
Develop and maintain a partner relationship log
Contribute to EDI training and equity audits of DAT programs
Travel to at least one in-field community engagement residency
who_you_are: Deeply committed to equitable, community-rooted practice
A thoughtful listener and communicator
Background in community organizing, social work, education, or arts-based community work
requirements: Experience in community engagement, organizing, or related field
Willing to travel for at least one residency

title: Stage Management / Production Apprentice
description: A 10-month apprenticeship for stage managers and production practitioners. Work across DAT's active productions — managing rehearsal processes, coordinating logistics, and building a career in international theatre production.
long_description: The Stage Management/Production Apprentice works with DAT's producing team to manage the production logistics of active projects — calling rehearsals, building and maintaining schedules, coordinating with designers and technical staff, and serving as the operational spine of at least one production. You'll work in unusual spaces, across cultures, and often without a full technical infrastructure. If you bring rigor to the unexpected, we want to know about you.
what_youll_do: Serve as stage manager or ASM on at least one active DAT production
Build and maintain production schedules, call sheets, and run-of-show documents
Coordinate with designers, technical staff, and artists
Manage rehearsal processes and communication flow
Travel in-field for at least one production
Mentor Production Interns
who_you_are: 1–3 years of stage management or production experience
Precise, organized, and calm under pressure
Comfortable working in non-traditional and international settings
requirements: Demonstrated stage management or production experience
Willing to travel internationally for at least one production

title: Documentation Apprentice
description: A 10-month apprenticeship for photographers, videographers, and documentary storytellers. Build DAT's archive while developing your documentary practice at the intersection of art, community, and theatre.
long_description: The Documentation Apprentice builds DAT's visual and narrative archive while developing their own documentary practice. You'll photograph, film, and narrate DAT's work — in rehearsal, in community, in the field — and work with the Comms team to shape that material into stories, reels, and a permanent institutional record.
what_youll_do: Document active DAT productions, workshops, and community sessions
Build a visual archive for each project — photos, video, audio
Collaborate with the Comms team on content production for social and digital channels
Develop a personal documentary series about a DAT project or community
Travel in the field for at least one ACTion expedition or residency
Contribute to DAT's permanent institutional archive
who_you_are: A documentary photographer, videographer, or multimedia storyteller
Comfortable working in community and performance settings
Thoughtful about the ethics of documentation and representation
requirements: Portfolio of documentary photography or video required

timeline.label: Field Work
timeline.detail: At least one expedition within program year

title: Devising / Ensemble Apprentice
description: A 10-month artist-development apprenticeship for ensemble performers and devisors. Work inside DAT's creative process, develop your devising practice, and perform in at least one project during the season.
long_description: The Devising/Ensemble Apprentice is an artist-development placement for performers and devisors ready to deepen their ensemble practice. You'll work inside DAT's creative process — learning DAT's devising methods, participating in rehearsals and workshops, contributing to original work, and performing in at least one production during the season. This is a rigorous, immersive apprenticeship designed for artists who are ready to grow.
what_youll_do: Participate in DAT ensemble rehearsals and devising sessions
Contribute to the creation of original material
Perform in at least one production during the program year
Receive mentorship from DAT's core artistic team
Lead a workshop or session for PLX Interns
Travel to at least one in-field ACTion or residency
who_you_are: A performer or devisor with 1–3 years of experience in ensemble or devised work
Collaborative, generous, and comfortable in the unknown
Ready to make real work, not just observe it
requirements: Comfortable with physical, vocal, and storytelling-based work

title: Dramaturgy / Adaptation Apprentice
description: A 10-month literary and dramaturgical apprenticeship for researchers, playwrights, and script developers. Work inside DAT's adaptation and devising process, building scripts, research archives, and your own dramaturgical practice.
long_description: The Dramaturgy/Adaptation Apprentice works with DAT's artistic team on the textual and research infrastructure of active productions — adapting source material, building dramaturgical archives, contributing to script development, and developing their own literary practice. This is an ideal placement for playwrights, dramaturgs, literary managers, and script developers who want to make work in community-engaged, international contexts.
what_youll_do: Conduct dramaturgical research for active productions and adaptations
Contribute to script development and adaptation of source material
Build and maintain production dramaturgical archives
Co-facilitate story research sessions with the artistic team
Contribute to at least one new text or adaptation during the program year
Travel to at least one in-field project for on-the-ground research
who_you_are: A playwright, dramaturg, or literary practitioner with 1–3 years of experience
Strong research and writing skills
Genuinely interested in cross-cultural storytelling and community-rooted work

title: Traveling Ensemble Artist Roster
description: Join DAT's artist bench for future international ensemble projects. Paid when placed on a project; rate varies by project scope and funding. This is an ongoing roster — not a call for a specific project.
compensation: Paid when placed; rate varies by project and funding
long_description:
DAT builds ensembles of traveling artists for each major international project — PASSAGE, ACTion, and CASTAWAY productions. When a new project opens, we draw from this roster to invite artists into the ensemble. Placement is project-specific and based on artistic fit, availability, and the needs of each community we’re working with.

This listing is for the ongoing evergreen roster. If you’re looking for the current open call — PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 — see that listing directly.
what_youll_do: Devise and perform original work with an international ensemble
Co-lead community workshops with local youth and community members
Adapt your practice to non-traditional performance spaces and cultural contexts
Contribute to documentation and storytelling for the project
Participate in post-residency sharings and presentations
who_you_are: An experienced ensemble performer or devisor
Comfortable working across language and culture
A listener first — genuinely interested in other people’s stories
Ready to be changed by the work
requirements: Experience in devised, physical, or community-engaged performance
Comfortable living and working in ensemble housing
Available to commit to the full in-field window of any project you’re placed on
perks: Paid when placed; artist fee rate confirmed upon engagement
Housing and logistics supported for the in-field period

timeline.label: Applications Reviewed
timeline.detail: Rolling — we review when new projects open

timeline.label: Project-Specific Timelines
timeline.detail: Shared directly when a match is made

faq.q: What’s the difference between this roster and PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026?
faq.a: PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 is a specific upcoming project with an artist participation fee model — you pay to participate. The Traveling Ensemble Artist Roster is for future paid ensemble projects where DAT places artists on contract. Apply to both if you’re interested in either.
We build the piece together from scratch — no pre-existing script. Come with stories, movement, and questions.

faq.q: How often do ensemble projects happen?
faq.a: Roughly one to two international ensemble projects per season. DAT is expanding its project calendar as it builds toward Season 21.

title: PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 — Traveling Artist
description: Travel with a small international ensemble across Slovakia — Bratislava, Košice, Zemplínska Teplica, and Slovenský Raj — July 12–August 2, 2026. Create original work shaped by place, encounter, and collective imagination. An artist participation fee applies.
compensation: Artist participation fee applies; amount shared with accepted applicants
long_description:
PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 is an immersive ensemble experience — part artistic journey, part cultural exchange, part creative development process, part collective making. A small international ensemble travels together through Slovakia, making original work shaped by place, encounter, conversation, and imagination.

The journey runs July 12–August 2, 2026, moving through Bratislava, Košice, Zemplínska Teplica, and Slovenský Raj. The project culminates in a final shared performance on August 1 in Košice.

PASSAGE is not a paid artist gig. It operates on an artist participation fee model — similar to an international residency or intensive, but in motion and in community. You contribute to the cost of the ensemble experience; what you make and carry back is yours. DAT organizes the ensemble, the logistics, the community partnerships, and the creative framework. You bring your craft, your curiosity, and your willingness to be changed by what you find.
what_youll_do: Travel with a small international ensemble through four regions of Slovakia
Make original devised work shaped by place, community encounters, and the journey itself
Participate in workshops, cultural exchanges, and reflection sessions throughout
Contribute to the final shared performance on August 1 in Košice
Document your process through writing, image, or your own artistic lens
Engage genuinely with local artists, youth, and community members along the route
who_you_are: A practicing artist — performer, deviser, writer, maker — with ensemble experience
Curious and adaptable across cultural and linguistic contexts
A listener first — genuinely interested in other people’s stories
Ready to make work in motion, without a fixed script or known outcome
Comfortable with shared living and ensemble travel
requirements: Valid passport with 6+ months remaining beyond August 2026
Ability to commit to the full in-field window: July 12 – August 2, 2026
Artist participation fee (amount shared with accepted applicants; payment plan available)
Experience in devised, physical, or community-engaged performance preferred
perks: 22 days of ensemble travel through an extraordinary cultural landscape
Original work made with and for real communities
Portfolio documentation of the journey and final performance
Creative credit on all PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 documentation

faq.q: Is this a paid artist job?
faq.a: No. PASSAGE operates on an artist participation fee model — you contribute to the cost of the ensemble experience. Think of it like an international residency or intensive, but in motion. The fee amount is shared with accepted applicants.

faq.q: What’s included in the participation fee?
faq.a: In-country logistics, shared accommodation, ground transport, and production costs for the final performance. International travel to/from Slovakia is not included.

faq.q: Do I need to speak Slovak?
faq.a: No. The ensemble works in English with local translation support. Curiosity and warmth carry further than language here.

faq.q: What kind of work will we make?
faq.a: Devised, site-responsive, ensemble work — built from scratch on the road. No pre-existing script. Come with stories, questions, and a practice that can travel.
Yes. Ensemble housing is shared throughout the journey. If that’s a constraint, reach out and we’ll talk through the options.

title: Actor / Devising Performer
description: Actors and devising performers for DAT's international productions, ACTion expeditions, and CASTAWAY co-productions. Open call for the general roster — applied to productions as they open.
compensation: Paid when placed; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT casts from a standing roster of actors and devising performers — pulled when the right project opens. Join the roster with a strong bio, work samples, and a note about your practice. We cast across genres, training backgrounds, and career stages. What we care about: ensemble instinct, story generosity, and the ability to work in the unknown.
what_youll_do: Submit bio, headshot/reel, and a short note on your practice
Get matched to relevant casting calls
Audition or interview when a project fits
Join an ensemble, make something together

Comfortable with physical, text-based, or hybrid devising approaches
Genuinely interested in community-engaged and international contexts
requirements: Passport and ability to travel internationally preferred
perks: Invitation to DAT artist events and gatherings

title: Director / Facilitator
description: Directors and facilitators for DAT's devised productions, ACTion expeditions, and community-engaged residencies. General roster — matched to projects as they open.
compensation: Paid when placed; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT works with directors and facilitators who can hold a devising room, build community trust, and navigate the beautiful complexity of making original work across cultures. If you have a practice at the intersection of directing and facilitation — and if you're drawn to international, community-rooted work — we want to know about you.
what_youll_do: Submit portfolio, bio, and a note on your practice
Get matched to directing and facilitation opportunities as they open
Work with DAT's artistic team to shape and direct an original piece
Lead ensemble and community facilitation sessions

Culturally humble, flexible, and at ease in the unknown
Strong listener and room-holder

title: Playwright / Adaptor
description: Playwrights and adaptors for DAT's original productions and CASTAWAY co-productions. General roster — matched to commissions and adaptation projects as they open.
compensation: Commission fee; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT commissions original scripts and adaptations for its productions — often derived from community stories, historical research, and in-the-field devising. We're building a roster of playwrights and adaptors who understand community-engaged dramaturgical practice and who can work inside a collaborative creative process. If you write from listening, we want to know about you.
what_youll_do: Submit portfolio and a note on your practice
Get matched to commission and adaptation opportunities
Collaborate with the artistic team on source material and development
Attend in-field research or residency when relevant
Deliver draft texts on production timelines

Comfortable with collaborative development and dramaturgical revision
Strong voice, strong ear, strong process

title: Dramaturg / Researcher
description: Dramaturgs and researchers for DAT's active productions and residencies. Deep knowledge of cultural context, world theatre, or community-based practice especially valued.
compensation: Paid when placed; rate varies by project
long_description:
DAT's productions are research-rich — built from history, community stories, cultural context, and textual adaptation. We work with dramaturgs who can hold the intellectual and ethical weight of that research, translate it for an ensemble, and stay inside the creative process as the work evolves.

If you have deep knowledge of Central Europe, Latin America, East Africa, or another DAT hub region, we especially want to hear from you.
what_youll_do: Conduct contextual research for a specific production or residency
Build and maintain a dramaturgical archive
Present research to the ensemble in accessible, actionable form
Advise on cultural accuracy, representation, and ethical storytelling
Attend rehearsals or workshops when relevant
who_you_are: A dramaturg or researcher with experience in community-engaged or world-theatre contexts
Intellectually rigorous and creatively flexible
Committed to ethical representation of community stories
requirements: Language skills or deep cultural knowledge of DAT's hub regions is a significant plus

title: Teaching Artist
description: Teaching artists for DAT's Drama Clubs, school residencies, and community storytelling programs. We place teaching artists locally and internationally — across NYC, Slovakia, Ecuador, and beyond.
commitment: Varies by project (semester, residency, or workshop-based)
compensation: Teaching artist fee; rate varies by program
long_description:
DAT's teaching artist practice is the heartbeat of the organization — rooted in Drama Club seasons, school residencies, community storytelling workshops, and the training of local teaching artists in each hub we work in.

We place teaching artists who are genuine artists first, who bring their practice into rooms with young people and community members, and who understand the difference between teaching skills and opening possibility. If you are a teaching artist — or an artist who wants to develop a teaching practice — we want to know about you.
what_youll_do: Lead Drama Club sessions, school residencies, or community workshops
Design curriculum in collaboration with DAT's education team
Develop youth and community voices through storytelling, devising, and performance
Document your teaching practice for DAT's educational archive
Participate in DAT's teaching artist training and professional development
Travel to international hubs if relevant and available
who_you_are: An artist first — a practitioner with a real creative practice
Warm, patient, and energized by working with young people and communities
Comfortable in both structured and open-ended educational settings
Interested in developing and refining your teaching artist pedagogy
requirements: Experience as a teaching artist or artist-educator
Background check required for youth-serving programs
Passport and willingness to travel for international placements

timeline.label: Placement
timeline.detail: When the right program opening matches your profile

faq.a: Helpful but not required. We train you on DAT's curriculum frameworks.
Yes. Travel and housing are included for international residency placements.

title: Local Artist / Cultural Collaborator
description: Local artists, cultural practitioners, and community members who partner with DAT's visiting ensembles. Based in one of DAT's hub cities — Slovakia, Ecuador, Tanzania, or beyond — and open to making work across cultures.
compensation: Collaboration fee; rate varies by project
long_description:
DAT's work only works because of local artists and cultural collaborators. When we arrive in Bratislava, Quito, or Bagamoyo, we come to listen — and local artists are the people we listen to first and most.

This call is for artists, cultural practitioners, community leaders, and creative people who are based in a DAT hub region and open to collaborating with an international ensemble. The relationship is reciprocal and honest.
what_youll_do: Introduce DAT's visiting ensemble to your community, practice, and place
Co-create material for an original devised piece
Lead or co-lead a community workshop from your own practice
Serve as a cultural guide and creative collaborator throughout the residency
Perform in or co-present the final work if relevant and desired
who_you_are: A local artist, cultural practitioner, community leader, or creative person based in a DAT hub region
Genuinely interested in cross-cultural creative exchange
Comfortable in collaborative and ensemble settings
requirements: Based in or deeply connected to a DAT hub region
Ability to communicate in English (with support) or in the local language with a translator

timeline.label: Matched
timeline.detail: When the right project in your region opens

faq.q: Which regions does DAT currently work in?
faq.a: Slovakia/Czechia, Ecuador, Tanzania, and NYC are our primary active hubs. We're scouting Australia and other regions.

Our designers are creative problem-solvers who work with the space, not against it — and who find beauty in constraints. We're building a roster of designers across disciplines for our upcoming productions and residencies.
what_youll_do: Submit portfolio and design statement
Get matched to design opportunities on upcoming productions
Collaborate with the director and ensemble on design concept
Execute design within production constraints (budget, space, travel)

Resourceful and collaborative
Comfortable with ambiguity and design constraints

Experience designing for non-traditional spaces preferred

title: Stage Manager
description: Stage managers for DAT's productions — NYC performances, ACTion expeditions, and international residencies. Comfort with non-traditional spaces, devised process, and international work is essential.
compensation: SM fee; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT's stage managers are the production's center of gravity — holding the process together across devising, community workshops, technical rehearsals, and performance. We work in unusual spaces, across cultures, and often without a full technical infrastructure. If you bring rigor to the unexpected, we want to know about you.
what_youll_do: Stage manage rehearsals, community workshops, and performances
Build and maintain schedules, contact sheets, and run-of-show documents
Coordinate with designers, technical staff, and artists
Call the show, manage the deck, manage the process

description: Musicians, composers, and sound artists for DAT's productions and residencies. We work with live music, original composition, and sound design — often in community and site-specific contexts.
compensation: Artist fee; rate varies by project
long_description: Music and sound are central to DAT's work — not as decoration, but as language. We work with musicians who can compose, improvise, teach, and perform in the same breath, and with sound artists who understand the texture of a room, a community, a culture. We're building a roster of musicians, composers, and sound artists for our upcoming productions and residencies.

title: Movement Director / Choreographer
description: Movement directors and choreographers for DAT's devised productions and physical theatre work. We value movement practitioners who work from the body and the community — not just the stage picture.
compensation: Artist fee; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT's productions are physical — they live in the body before they live on the page. We work with movement directors and choreographers who can work in ensemble, across cultures, and with non-professional community members as well as trained performers. If your practice is rooted in the body as a storytelling instrument, we want to hear from you.
what_youll_do: Lead movement work with ensemble and community participants

title: Visual Artist / Maker / Artisan
description: Visual artists, makers, and artisans who want to collaborate with DAT on set, props, installations, or community-art projects. We especially value handcraft, site-specificity, and work made from local materials.
compensation: Artist fee; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT's productions live in real places — and we want the physical world of the show to live there too. We work with visual artists, makers, and artisans who can build set elements, props, installations, and community-art pieces from local materials, in local traditions, in collaboration with the people who live there. If you make things with your hands and you're curious about what art can do in a community, we want to hear from you.
what_youll_do: Collaborate with DAT's design team on visual and physical elements of a production
Source and work with local materials and artisans

title: Documentation Artist
description: Photographers, videographers, and documentary storytellers for DAT's productions and residencies. We want documentation that captures the truth of the work — not just the performance.
compensation: Artist fee; rate varies by project
long_description:
DAT's work happens in fields, studios, community centers, and living rooms around the world — and most of it is never seen by anyone outside the room. The Documentation Artist changes that.

We're building a roster of photographers, videographers, and documentary storytellers who want to capture the real texture of the work — the listening, the rehearsal, the moment before the show, the meal afterward. If you can make a frame that tells the truth, we want to hear from you.
what_youll_do: Document productions, workshops, and community sessions in the field
Build a project archive — photo, video, audio
Collaborate with the Comms team on content production
Develop your own documentary project around the work
Contribute to DAT's permanent institutional archive
who_you_are: A photographer, videographer, or documentary storyteller
Comfortable working in community and performance settings
Thoughtful about the ethics of documentation
requirements: Portfolio of documentary photography or video
Ability to travel in-field for applicable projects

title: Board Committee Member
description: Serve on a DAT Board committee — Finance, Governance, Development, or Artistic Advisory. Governance-level engagement without full Board commitment. For people who believe in DAT’s mission and bring relevant expertise.
long_description:
DAT’s Board committees are where mission meets expertise. We’re building committees in Finance, Governance, Development, and Artistic Advisory — each a focused group of people who bring real knowledge and care to DAT’s institutional work.

Committee members are not full Board members. They advise, review, and contribute on specific questions — without the full fiduciary commitment of Board service. This is a meaningful, low-overhead way to support an organization doing unusual work.
what_youll_do: Attend committee meetings (typically 4–6 per year, virtual)
Review materials relevant to your committee’s focus (finance reports, governance docs, grant proposals, etc.)
Provide expert input, ask hard questions, and help DAT think clearly
Connect DAT staff to relevant contacts in your network where appropriate
Support the full Board’s decision-making with committee recommendations
who_you_are: A professional with expertise in finance, law, governance, development, or the arts
Genuinely interested in DAT’s mission and the sector it works in
Willing to engage seriously with the organization’s real challenges
No formal governance or Board experience required — orientation provided
requirements: Ability to attend virtual committee meetings (approximately monthly)
perks: Meaningful governance engagement without full Board commitment
DAT community access and invitations to programs

timeline.label: Rolling
timeline.detail: Join a committee anytime — next meeting scheduled upon onboarding

faq.a: Finance, Governance, Development, and Artistic Advisory. Tell us your background and we’ll suggest the right fit.

faq.q: Does this lead to a full Board seat?
faq.a: It can, but it doesn’t have to. Many committee members stay as committee members indefinitely.

title: Host Committee Member
description: Lend your name, network, and enthusiasm to a DAT event, campaign, or fundraiser. Help us reach people we wouldn’t otherwise reach — by making a personal ask, sharing the event, or simply showing up.
long_description:
DAT’s host committees are loose, event-specific networks of people who believe in the work and are willing to say so publicly. For each major event or campaign, we invite a small group to lend their names, share the event with their networks, and make a personal ask to a few people they think would care.

This is not a standing commitment. Each host committee is formed for a specific purpose and dissolves when the event or campaign ends. You opt in each time.
what_youll_do: Lend your name to a specific DAT event, fundraiser, or campaign
Share the event with people in your network via email or personal message
Make a personal ask to two or three people you think would genuinely respond
Attend the event or campaign launch where possible
who_you_are: Someone who believes in DAT’s work and is willing to say so
Connected to people who might attend, donate, or get involved
Comfortable making a personal (not mass) ask on behalf of something you believe in
requirements: No formal requirements — this is about your enthusiasm and your network
Willingness to make a personal ask to at least two or three people
perks: Invitations to all DAT events you help host

timeline.label: Rolling
timeline.detail: We invite host committee members on a per-event basis — let us know you’re interested and we’ll reach out when the next one comes together

faq.a: No. Each host committee is event-specific. You opt in each time — no ongoing obligation.

faq.q: Do I need to make a donation?
faq.a: Not required, but appreciated. The most valuable thing is your personal outreach to people who wouldn’t otherwise hear about DAT.

title: Drama Club Teaching Artist Lead
description: Lead DAT’s teaching artist work in an active Drama Club hub — Slovakia or Ecuador. Facilitate original storytelling sessions with young people, mentor local teaching artists, and help a community realize that their stories matter. Project-based contract.
compensation: Project-based contract fee; rate shared with qualified applicants
long_description:
DAT’s Drama Clubs exist because young people deserve space to tell their own stories — and because communities are stronger when those stories are heard.

The Drama Club Teaching Artist Lead facilitates DAT’s storytelling and devising process with youth participants in an active hub (currently Slovakia and Ecuador). You’ll run sessions, mentor local co-facilitators, and help young people develop original work rooted in their own lives and imagination.

This is hands-on, relational, creative work. It requires patience, adaptability, genuine curiosity about young people’s stories, and a practice grounded in community rather than performance outcome.
what_youll_do: Facilitate original storytelling and devising sessions with youth participants
Adapt DAT’s methodology to the local context, culture, and age group
Mentor and co-facilitate with local teaching artists and community co-leads
Guide participants from story collection through to performance or sharing
Document sessions and report on participant development to DAT program staff
Coordinate with DAT on session design, arc, and community relationships
who_you_are: An experienced teaching artist with a genuine practice in devising or storytelling
Patient, adaptable, and skilled at building trust with young people
Comfortable working across language and culture — ideally with prior cross-cultural experience
Motivated by community impact, not just performance credits
Willing to listen more than you speak, at least at first
requirements: 2+ years of teaching artist or youth theatre facilitation experience
Experience with devised or community-engaged practice
Ability to commit to the full Drama Club session arc
References from prior teaching artist or community work
For Slovakia hubs: comfort with Central European context; for Ecuador: Spanish proficiency strongly preferred
perks: Project-based contract fee (rate discussed with qualified applicants)
Housing support for non-local hires where applicable

timeline.label: Inquire Now
timeline.detail: Season 21 Drama Clubs planned for 2026–2027; engagement timing to be confirmed

timeline.label: Target Engagement
timeline.detail: Fall 2026 or Spring 2027 depending on hub

faq.a: Slovakia and Ecuador are our current active Drama Club hubs. Other hubs may open as DAT builds toward Season 21.

faq.q: Do I need to speak Slovak or Spanish?
faq.a: Slovak is helpful but not required for Slovakia hubs — we work with local co-facilitators and translators. Spanish proficiency is strongly preferred for Ecuador.
